TextENGNew: It started from nothing more than a mischievous impulse.
When I arrived at the clinic with a present for Satoko... I figured the front entrance would be locked, so I decided to go around back.
The truth is, I'd noticed while visiting the clinic recently that the lock on the window of Satoko's examination room was broken. With just a little pressure on the narrow gap, it would swing open.
I felt a pang of guilt about sneaking in uninvited, but I wanted to place this cute stuffed animal at Satoko's bedside as soon as possible. That desire overruled any moral or common-sense objections.
...That's right -- at the time, I was strangely elated, even by my own standards. The afterglow of all the fun I'd had with everyone at the festival was surely part of it.
In that state, I happened to glance ahead... and there, half-hidden in the darkness, I could make out Ooishi's silhouette, dimly illuminated by the glow of his cigarette.
``...Why is Ooishi here?``
Given the hour and the special circumstances of the Watanagashi night, it was possible he was out on village patrol.
...But something about the way he stood there in the darkness -- his normally imposing, stout frame seeming to lack its usual strength and spirit -- told me that wasn't the case.
``...Oh well, never mind that.``
I crept around to the back of the clinic, keeping my footsteps silent.
Just as I'd expected, the window to that room was slightly ajar. One firm tug and it would open right up. I gently set the stuffed animal on the ground and placed my hands on the window frame...
``...In which case, we'll have to move Satoshi-kun the same way.``
There were people inside... but before that thought even registered, the words that had just reached my ears hit me with such force that my entire body froze.
That voice... it was unmistakably the Coach.
And... what did he just say...?
``...You mean Satoko's older brother, Satoshi Houjou?!@k@vS14/01/220100090. So Satoshi has been... here in this clinic?!``
``...I'm sorry.@k@vS14/05/220500157. I still haven't told Keiichi and the others about Satoshi.``
``I-Is that so...?``
I could hear them.
Not just the Coach -- Kei-chan and Rika-chama were in there too, having a conversation with him.
I could hear every detail, right down to the Coach's weary sighs.
Then... what I heard just now... it wasn't a mistake...?
``...I don't know if 'alive' is the right word.@k@vS14/10/22100154. But Satoshi-kun has played an enormously important role in our research.``
``It's no exaggeration to say that he's the reason we were able to save Satoko-chan.``
What... do you mean by that, Coach?
Why can't you just say that Satoshi-kun is alive?
That means... no, it can't be...!

``In the old days of Hinamizawa, they used to conduct experiments on human bodies in the name of research.@k@vS14/09/220900002. Apparently, they were dissected and used as specimens to analyze the various components contained within their cells.``
``...Among the people who went missing from time to time, it seems some were sent away for those experiments.``
That can't be true...?
Then... Satoshi-kun was sent away for those 'experiments'...?
Please... someone, tell me it's a lie.
And of all people... the Coach -- someone so kind and gentle -- was involved in this!
......Ah. I see. So that's how it is.
The Coach had been eyeing Satoshi-kun like that from the very beginning.
Feigning sympathy, getting close... all to investigate him as a research subject.
Satoshi-kun was already on bad terms with the villagers. In such a lonely, painful, broken state... if someone offered kind words, of course he'd open up.
And then, using the curse as a convenient cover, they spirited him away. And then... and then... they used him!!
That's right. Of course. It has to be.
Even if Satoshi-kun disappeared, nobody would bat an eye. In fact, most people would probably be relieved to see him gone.
And Rika-chan -- she knew where Satoshi-kun was this whole time and pretended she didn't?
Then... Mion knew about this too...?!
``...That can't be...!!``
If that's the case... then Satoko is next?!
It's possible. Come to think of it, it's strange that she's been kept in this clinic all this time when she supposedly just fainted. They must be using some kind of drug to keep her asleep.
changeReason: New translation adds location detail not in original: 'clinic'.

TextENGNew: ...Could they be planning to use tonight's Watanagashi to go after Satoko?
I won't let them. I won't let that happen.
I couldn't protect Satoshi-kun back then. That was because I wasn't vigilant enough, because I didn't try hard enough.
But this time is different. I will protect her, no matter what. Because that was my promise to Satoshi-kun.
``Satoko... I'm counting on you.``
Yeah... I know, Satoshi-kun.
You trusted me and entrusted Satoko to my care.
So I'll protect her.
I won't hand Satoko over to some hypocritical mad scientist.
So watch me... Satoshi-kun...!

...And so I crept into Mion's room at the Sonozaki main house. Mion was naturally asleep, but since we'd promised to spend the night together, she merely stirred slightly at my presence before drifting back to sleep.
Taking care not to make a sound, I found the modified model gun that Mion always kept on her desk... and quietly slid open the drawer to retrieve the ammunition.
She'd been carrying it less frequently since meeting Keiichi, but it had more than enough stopping power -- and if caught with it loaded, a run-in with the police would be guaranteed.
After checking the safety, I stuffed it into my back pocket. Then I slipped out of Mion's room...
``I'll ask you one more time.@k@vS14/06/220600016. Where is Satoshi-kun?``
Irie gulped, unnerved by the murderous look on Shion's face.

The muzzle pointed at him still reeked of gunpowder from the earlier shot. The menace of that dark, gleaming weapon wasn't lost on him, but what unsettled him even more was the terrifyingly intense look in Shion's upturned eyes -- a look that went beyond mere fear.
Could it be... that Shion's Hinamizawa Syndrome was flaring up...?
``Sh-Shion-san... where did you hear about that...?``

Another gunshot thundered through the room, and the window behind him shattered, sending glass shards flying outward. Shion's expression didn't so much as flicker -- there wasn't even a tremor of hesitation left in her eyes.
``...I believe I asked first.@k@vS14/06/220600018. Answer my question before asking your own.@k@vS14/06/220600019. Where is Satoshi-kun?``
``...W-Well, that's...``

``...Let me be perfectly clear: if you think I won't pull this trigger, you're sorely mistaken.@k@vS14/06/220600021. If I decide you're not going to talk, I'll put a bullet through your skull without a second thought. Keep that in mind.@k@vS14/06/220600022. And then I'll simply search this place on my own.``
Shion declared this coldly. ...And as if to underscore her words, she shifted the muzzle from Irie's face to his chest with a slow, deliberate motion.
If your intent is to shoot someone, the chest is the easiest target. Especially if you're not aiming to kill in a single shot.
``...For now, raise both your hands to show me you understand.@k@vS14/06/220600024. Then put them behind your head.``
...Shion was dead serious. So Irie did as instructed without wasting words, raising both hands.
``Shion-san...``
``I don't need any explanations.@k@vS14/06/220600026. Don't speak unless I ask you something.@k@vS14/06/220600027. ...Now -- lead me to where Satoshi-kun is.``
Shion moved behind Irie and pressed the gun barrel against his back. Then, with a firm jab, she prodded him forward.
``...Oh, that's right.@k@vS14/06/220600029. Disable the security system, would you?@k@vS14/06/220600030. You do have one here, don't you?``
``...Very well.``
They exited the examination room and headed to the door at the end of the hallway. Behind it lay a staircase leading to the second floor.
...But Irie bypassed it, instead moving aside the cardboard boxes stacked behind the stairs. There, previously hidden, a second staircase descended into darkness below.
``...Satoshi-kun is down there?``
``That's right.@k@vS14/10/22100163. ...Shion-san, I know that no amount of apology could ever make up for keeping this from you.``
``But... honestly, I don't think going any further will do you any good.@k@vS14/10/22100165. So...``
``One more word and I shoot. I mean it.``
Shion's face was utterly merciless as her thumb clicked the hammer into position. Her index finger rested on the trigger, the muzzle still pressed firmly against Irie's back.
If Shion's emotions surged even slightly and her finger tightened, a hole would literally be blown through Irie's torso.
...And even if he died here, Shion would never stop pressing forward. But what awaited her wasn't a joyful reunion -- it was, by any measure, the worst possible outcome.
``...We're going down.@k@vS14/10/22100167. Watch your step.``

Peering over Irie's shoulder, she could make out a door at the bottom, dimly visible in the faint light. ...Shion kept a wary eye on Irie as she carefully descended the stairs, making sure not to lose her footing.
Their footsteps echoed -- two sets, tap, tap. ...And as they drew closer to the door, the silence gave way to the low, grating hum of machinery trembling through the air.
Dread and anxiety rose steadily from the pit of her stomach. And to smother those feelings, Shion unconsciously amplified her hatred and killing intent toward Irie.
Irie opened the door, and Shion pushed him through ahead of her.
What she saw left her momentarily stunned. ...The space was larger than the entire ground floor, with a central corridor flanked by numerous rooms on either side.
The interior was lit only by dim emergency lighting. Once inside, she could clearly hear the electronic chirps and beeps of what was presumably medical equipment.
It was less like a hospital ward... and more like a morgue.
``...Satoshi-kun...``
She clenched her jaw so hard it felt as though her molars might shatter.
They had locked Satoshi-kun away in a place like this.
This cold, dark, damp place where the only sound was the hateful drone of machines. Unforgivable, unforgivable, unforgivable, unforgivable, unforgivable, unforgivable...!!
``So you had this whole floor built down here...@k@vS14/06/220600035. This clinic gets more suspicious by the minute.``
Shion tossed out the quip, but Irie remained silent, apparently still adhering to her earlier command.
His dogged obedience only irritated her more, and she clicked her tongue. ...Besides, the skin on her neck and wrists had been maddeningly itchy for a while now.
When she looked up, her eyes met Irie's as he glanced back over his shoulder. ...Those eyes. She hated them. They looked pitying, condescending, mocking, sneering...!
She'd lost count of how many times she'd wanted to shoot him.
Still, killing Irie here would make finding Satoshi that much harder. ...She'd said she could search alone, but wandering through a place she knew nothing about posed considerable risk.
Eventually, Irie stopped before a door at the very end of the corridor. He turned slowly to face Shion behind him.
``...He's in here.``
She couldn't contain herself. Shion shoved Irie aside, threw herself at the glass window set into the door, and peered desperately inside.
...Inside was a bed... and lying in it, with an IV drip attached to his arm, was Satoshi, still asleep.

Electrodes for measuring brainwaves were affixed to his forehead and chest, and the monitor they fed into traced undulating waveforms of light. ...At the very least, it was proof that he was still alive.
She had braced herself.

When she'd heard Irie say he wasn't sure if Satoshi could be called 'alive'... she had steeled herself for the worst -- the possibility of finding 'Satoshi' reduced to something like an anatomical specimen in a science lab.
But... Satoshi was there. Just as she remembered him.
The relief was overwhelming. In that moment, it was the only thing that gave her any comfort.
``Satoshi-kun... Satoshi-kun...!!``
But his sleep seemed impossibly deep. ...His face was so deathly pale that without being told he was alive, one might not have believed it.
``...H-He's alive, right...?@k@vS14/06/220600042. Satoshi-kun is still alive, right...?!``
Before long, realizing that shouting was useless, Shion grabbed the doorknob and wrenched it violently back and forth, trying to force her way in.
But she spotted the card-reader lock mounted on the wall beside the door, and spun back toward Irie, leveling the gun at him once more.
``Open this door! Now!!@k@vS14/06/220600047. Open it already!!``
``Shion-san... please.@k@vS14/10/22100171. Shoot me if you must.@k@vS14/10/22100172. But there is one thing -- just one thing -- I need you to promise me.``
Even now, Irie was presenting conditions to Shion. ...Her eyes blazed with rage at his insistence on drawing things out.
``Please -- do not open this door.@k@vS14/10/22100174. ...And you must not try to wake him.@k@vS14/10/22100175. Do you understand? Please, promise me.``
``Wh-Why?!@k@vS14/06/220600049. Why can't I wake him up?!``
That was the very first thing Shion wanted to do. She wanted to be the one to tell him he was saved, that he was free...!! Being told she couldn't filled her with fury.
``Satoshi-kun... is ill.@k@vS14/10/22100177. Severely so -- to the point where a full recovery cannot be hoped for at present...``
``Excuse me?!@k@vS14/06/220600051. After coming all this way, you're spouting that nonsense?!@k@vS14/06/220600052. How many times do I have to tell you?! Open it now or I shoot!!``
``It's the truth!!@k@vS14/10/22100179. Please, believe at least that!!@k@vS14/10/22100180. I understand how you feel -- completely. But if you enter this room and see him, you will regret it!!``
A gunshot roared. Irie staggered where he stood.
...The bullet, fired as a warning, grazed his right arm. Fresh blood seeped from the torn flesh.

``...Next it'll be your leg, maybe?@k@vS14/06/220600054. Or your shoulder?@k@vS14/06/220600055. The stomach's always an option too...!@k@vS14/06/220600056. Now -- open it!!@k@vS14/06/220600057. Or would you prefer I put a hole through your belly right here in front of this door?!@k@vS14/06/220600058. Ahahahahaha!!!``
``...Shion-san...!``

``Don't look at me!!@k@vS14/06/220600060. Don't look at me with that face!!@k@vS14/06/220600061. It's infuriating!!@k@vS14/06/220600062. Come on!@k@vS14/06/220600063. Didn't you hear me? I said open it already--``
``Then shoot me!!@k@vS14/10/22100184. I've committed sins enough to deserve it, and you have every right!!``
``But you must not open this door!!@k@vS14/10/22100186. On my honor as a physician, I cannot allow another tragedy to unfold!!!``

The ferocity of Irie's roar -- something she had never witnessed before -- made Shion flinch, and her finger pulled the trigger involuntarily. The unaimed shot ricocheted off the floor and sliced a sharp cut across Irie's cheek.
A thin line of red blood trickled down. But Irie didn't shrink back. He fixed Shion with a steely, piercing gaze and stepped directly in front of the muzzle.
``This is painful to say, but I'll tell you only the truth, and briefly.@k@vS14/10/22100188. ...One year ago, Satoshi-kun suffered severe damage to his brain and psyche from accumulated stress.``
``...Fortunately, his life is not in danger. But... as he is now, Satoshi-kun cannot distinguish friend from foe, truth from delusion.``
``Th-Then... what does that mean...?!``

``He interprets everything around him as malicious... consumed by extreme paranoid delusions and terror... and in his desperation to escape, he will attack anyone in front of him, regardless of who they are.``
``...Last year, a careless staff member was injured so badly that she required reconstructive surgery.@k@vS14/10/22100192. ...He would not make an exception, even for you.``
It was only then that Shion noticed. ...Satoshi was covered with a blanket on the bed, but over it lay two belt-like straps, keeping him from getting up.
...And leather restraints circled his arms, securing him to the bed frame.
...Those leather restraints bore multiple scars and tears -- the marks of something violent.
``.........No...@k@vS14/06/220600067. Is he ever... going to get better...?!``
That silence... was unspeakably cruel.
...Irie wanted nothing more than to tell her firmly that he would recover. ...But he was a doctor. He knew that irresponsible words could ultimately cause deeper wounds. ...So he could say nothing.

``Wh-... What is that supposed to mean?@k@vS14/06/220600069. ......What... is that...@k@vS14/06/220600070. ...Then... what?@k@vS14/06/220600071. Satoshi-kun will never... say 'mmph' to me again...?@k@vS14/06/220600072. ...He'll never... pat my head again...?@k@vS14/06/220600073. Why?@k@vS14/06/220600074. ...Why...?!``
Shion pounded the glass with her fist, tears streaming down her face.
``You mustn't...@k@vS14/10/22100195. If you truly care about him...!@k@vS14/10/22100196. ...please, don't wake him...@k@vS14/10/22100197. I'm certain that's what he would want, too.``
``...If you wake him... the Satoshi-kun you see... will surely hurt you.``
``...And... Satoshi-kun himself... wouldn't want you to see him like that.@k@vS14/10/22100200. ...So please... try to understand...``
"......Th-That... ...can't be...@k@vS14/06/220600076. ......Ugh... Uwaaaaahhhhhhh...@k@vS14/06/220600077. ...Uwaaaaaahhhh... waaaaaahhhh..."
changeReason: Old renders そ、そんな as 'Wh-Why?' while JPN means 'Th-That... can't be'; the rest is similar wailing. Different initial reaction changes the emotional framing.

TextENGNew: Shion slid down the glass window, crumpling to the floor as sobs wracked her body.

She clawed at the floor... scratching at the tiles, driven by the anguish of this bitter reunion and a fury with nowhere to go. If Satoshi's smile had been buried beneath those tiles, Shion would have kept scratching until her fingernails tore off.
But... Satoshi wasn't under the floor. He was right there. ...Lying on the bed, locked in an endless sleep. Cradled in a slumber from which he must never be awakened...
And then, Shion...
...transformed the crushing grief in her chest into rage and hatred, and leveled the pistol at Irie once more.
``Why didn't you tell me...?!``
``When Satoshi-kun disappeared -- why didn't you tell me then?!@k@vS14/06/220600081. It wouldn't have mattered how much he'd changed, even if he'd gone mad, even if he could never speak again!``
``If I'd known Satoshi-kun was alive, I could have found comfort! I wouldn't have been so lost in grief! I could have held onto hope!!@k@vS14/06/220600083. So why?! Why did you keep it from me?!``
Irie simply stood there, dejected and motionless. ...Whether out of penitence or resignation, he showed no fear and made no attempt to flee.

``Do you have any idea what Satoko and I went through, waiting and waiting?!@k@vS14/06/220600085. How completely our hearts were torn apart?!@k@vS14/06/220600086. How much misery Satoko had to endure?!@k@vS14/06/220600087. You were right there beside her -- you saw it better than anyone!!``
``And yet... and yet you've been deceiving us all this time?!``
``Then if I had told you, what do you think would have happened?!``
Irie's expression had already settled into one of grim resolve.

``If I'd told you, you would have wanted to see him.@k@vS14/10/22100204. And once you saw him, you'd want to touch him.@k@vS14/10/22100205. You'd want to talk to him.@k@vS14/10/22100206. ...That wouldn't bring you happiness.@k@vS14/10/22100207. ...It would only deepen your pain.``
``Th-That's sophistry!@k@vS14/06/220600090. That's nothing but your own selfish assumption!!``
``I won't deny it... I underestimated the severity of his illness.@k@vS14/10/22100209. I thought I could cure him quickly, so... I planned to tell you once his condition improved.``
``If there was to be a reunion, I wanted both of you to be well.@k@vS14/10/22100211. ...But Satoshi-kun's consciousness never returned... and in the end, I lost the chance to tell you.``
``Don't make excuses!@k@vS14/06/220600092. You were enjoying it!@k@vS14/06/220600093. Watching us cry, grieve, and suffer -- all while putting on a sympathetic face for your own amusement!!``

``That was never my intention!@k@vS14/10/22100213. ...And yet, my own foolishness is what brought us to this moment.@k@vS14/10/22100214. ...When I think of that, I hate myself enough to wish I were dead.``
``Is that so... Then...!``
Shion raised the pistol with trembling hands. She had already reached her limit.

``Don't worry... I'll put you out of your misery...!@k@vS14/06/220600096. For what you did to Satoshi-kun -- and for the grudge Satoko and I bear against you!!@k@vS14/06/220600097. ...But first... there's one more thing... I want to ask...``
``That machine... what is it for?@k@vS14/06/220600099. What are you doing to Satoshi-kun's body...?``
``...To find a treatment for the pathogen, we're using the virus that has taken hold in Satoshi-kun to...@k@vS14/10/22100217. ...develop experimental reagents.``
``...!! You're doing that... to Satoshi-kun...!!!``
Overcome with fury, Shion tightened her finger on the trigger -- and in that instant.
``Shii-chan?!``
When we entered the examination room... the stuffed animal lying on the floor amid shattered glass made it immediately clear that something terrible had happened.
``Wh-What on earth happened here?@k@vS14/03/220300022. Where's the Coach?!``
``...Keiichi!!``
Slipping past the flustered Mion, Rika-chan -- now awake -- burst in and rushed to the bedside.
...Despite the chaos of the room, Satoko lay there peacefully, her sleeping face serene. Seeing that, we all breathed a sigh of relief.
``Satoko's all right!@k@vS14/01/220100093. ...But where's the Coach?``
``This is clearly from a gun.@k@vS14/03/220300024. ...And what's more...``
Mion picked up the stuffed animal lying on the floor. Her face had gone deathly pale.
``...This is the one Shion said she was going to give Satoko...``
``Then... Shion's here too...?``
``There's no time to think, Keiichi-kun!@k@vS14/02/220200019. We have to find them!!``
That we made it here at all was nothing short of a miracle.
The door to the stairwell had been left open by chance... and the collapsed cardboard boxes revealed the hidden passage beyond.
Seeing that, we'd meant to split up -- one group for the basement, one for the second floor. But in our agitation, all of us rushed downstairs together.
``Shii-chan?!``
As it turned out, that worked in our favor. Sharp-eyed Rena was the first to spot someone standing before a door at the far end of the corridor, aiming a gun at the back of a man in a white coat.
We sprinted down the hallway, and once my eyes finally adjusted to the dim light, I recognized the man in the lab coat as the Coach. I shouted.
``Wh-What are you doing, Shion?!``
``Don't come any closer!!@k@vS14/06/220600103. Nobody move!!``
Even though it was only a warning shot, the gunshot reverberating through the enclosed space brought all of us to an immediate halt.

With her back pressed against the door at the far end, Shion held the gun in both hands, her face twisted into an expression that was terrifying even at this distance, as she menaced both the Coach and us.
``Everyone... you've all been lying to me, deceiving me!!@k@vS14/06/220600105. You've all been hiding Satoshi-kun from me, haven't you?!``
``Huuh?!@k@vS14/03/220300027. What are you talking about, Shion?!@k@vS14/03/220300028. What does Satoshi have to do with this?!``

``Don't play dumb, Mion!!@k@vS14/06/220600107. When you said we'd search for him together... I was so happy that you understood how I felt... so incredibly happy!!@k@vS14/06/220600108. Was all of that a lie too?!!``
``I-I don't understand...!@k@vS14/03/220300030. Why... why are you saying these things?@k@vS14/03/220300031. Why won't you believe me?!``
``Mii... I'm sorry.@k@vS14/05/220500160. This is... my fault.``
Rika-chan tugged at the sleeve of the half-crying Mion, stopping her. Then, slowly stepping forward past all of us, she moved to stand alongside the Coach.

``Shii.@k@vS14/05/220500162. ...Mii truly didn't know.@k@vS14/05/220500163. Of everyone here, the only ones who knew were Irie and me.@k@vS14/05/220500164. So please -- the others have nothing to do with this.``
``R-Rika-san...?``

``Rika-chama...!@k@vS14/06/220600110. So you were in league with this villain all along?!@k@vS14/06/220600111. With this man who's been toying with Satoshi-kun's body, conducting human experiments!!``
``...S-Satoshi... What do you mean?!@k@vS14/03/220300034. He's... here?``
``H-Human experiments...? Rika-chan, what's going on?@k@vS14/02/220200022. I don't understand any of this -- please explain!!``
Rika-chan looked back over her shoulder with a pained expression at the bewildered Mion and Rena. Seeing her face, something clicked for me, and I spoke up.
``...In that room behind Shion...@k@vS14/01/220100099. Satoshi is... in there...?``
``R-Really?!@k@vS14/03/220300036. Satoshi is alive?!@k@vS14/03/220300037. He's right there in that room?!``
``Can you really call that being alive?!``

``Being treated like this -- a test subject for experimental drugs?!@k@vS14/06/220600114. Who authorized this? Who approved it? Who said this was acceptable?!@k@vS14/06/220600115. How dare you use Satoshi-kun's body for something so inhumane!!``
``...If we're talking about test subjects, I'm one too.@k@vS14/05/220500167. Both Satoko and I are cooperating as test subjects to support the research into Hinamizawa Syndrome.``
``In order to understand the true nature of this disease, it's necessary to verify the effectiveness of Irie's new drugs on living humans.``
``Whaaat...?!``
Mion and Rena stood speechless, overwhelmed by this cascade of words they'd never expected to hear.
I'd at least been briefed on the endemic disease beforehand, but learning that even Satoko was involved in the research caught me off guard.
``Then... even Satoko...?``
``Satoko doesn't know.@k@vS14/05/220500170. ...I'll tell you everything now. At one point, Satoko fell into the terminal stages of Hinamizawa Syndrome.``
``The only way to save her life was to enroll her in this project and continue administering experimental drugs.``
``...Why didn't you tell us?@k@vS14/01/220100102. This is the first I've heard of any of this!``
``...What triggered Satoko's illness was a deeply tragic accident.@k@vS14/05/220500173. If I could help it, I never wanted to tell Keiichi, or Shii... or anyone who cares about Satoko.``
``...Three years ago, she couldn't adjust to her new father no matter how hard she tried. The stress of daily life was eating away at her.``
``You don't mean... that...?``
At Mion's question, Rika-chan lowered her gaze. As if to shoulder the burden of her pain, the Coach spoke up in her place.
``It was three years ago, during Watanagashi.@k@vS14/10/22100220. Satoko-chan's parents took her on a drive, but...@k@vS14/10/22100221. at the cliff where they arrived, there was a fatal fall.``
``The police ruled it an accident caused by deteriorated railings... but our investigation strongly suggests they were pushed by someone.``
``...And based on eyewitness reports from near the scene, the only people present were... the Houjou couple and Satoko-chan.``
``That's a lie!!!``
Unable to bear it any longer, Shion screamed.
``So you're saying Satoko killed her own parents?!``
``Let me tell you something -- Satoko is not that kind of girl!@k@vS14/06/220600119. She may love her pranks, but she's gentle and strong-willed... She knows right from wrong!!``

``No matter how much she hated them, no matter how much she might have wished them dead, Satoko would never do that!!@k@vS14/06/220600121. Don't just make up whatever suits you! I won't let you smear her name to wriggle out of this!!``
``...That is precisely what Hinamizawa Syndrome does.``
Rika-chan shook her head and spoke in a heavy, sorrowful voice.
``When someone is afflicted with Hinamizawa Syndrome, they come to believe that everyone around them harbors hostility toward them.``
``The smallest words or gestures breed suspicion, groundless paranoia, and anxiety.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1185. ...The overwhelming urge to protect oneself twists into violent, aggressive impulses.``
``...But Shion.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1187. You're right. It is not Satoko's fault.``
``She's a gentle, sweet girl who loves to be spoiled... and the disease merely drove her in a terrible direction.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1189. Both Irie and I understand that.``
``...That's why I wanted to save Satoko-chan.@k@vS14/10/22100225. I've been repeatedly administering medication and running tests toward that end, but the results haven't been encouraging...``

``On top of that, her symptoms made Satoko-chan increasingly hostile toward her new parents -- Teppei and his wife -- and their own poor handling of the situation only accelerated the deterioration.``
``...Then, a few days after last year's Watanagashi, I met with Satoshi-kun.``
``Satoshi-kun... I'll come clean. He confessed to me that he had killed his aunt.``
``Even so, he said he wanted to buy Satoko-chan a birthday present before turning himself in to the police. So I drove him to a toy store.``
``And... he bought a stuffed animal. We were on our way to the police station after that.``
``Satoshi-kun was sitting in the passenger seat... when he suddenly became violent.``
``He started throwing things and swinging at me while I was driving, and finally bared his teeth and bit me.``
``I desperately tried to restrain him.``
``...And when he started clawing frantically at his own throat, I realized it was the onset of Hinamizawa Syndrome.``
``So I had no choice but to sedate him with an anesthetic I happened to have on hand, and bring him to this facility.``
``I wanted to save him, no matter what.``
``I couldn't let Shion-san and Satoko-chan -- the people who cherished him -- suffer that kind of grief.``
``...But reality was cruel.``
``The tests revealed that Satoshi-kun's symptoms had reached Level 5.``
changeReason: Numerical quantities differ: old has none unique, new has {5}.

TextENGNew: ``Under normal protocol, that prognosis would be classified as terminal... and the manual would dictate euthanasia.``
``I agonized over it.``
``As things stood, there was no prospect of a human life of happiness for Satoshi-kun.``
``If that was the case, then perhaps it would be better to...``
``But at the same time, I wanted to cling to that slender thread of hope -- that future advances in medicine might find a way.``
``To that end, I persuaded the higher-ups and my staff, and confined him to this room under the pretext of using him as a test subject for experimental drugs... Yes. I locked him away.``
``But... it's ironic.``
``It was precisely because he existed as a sample that we were able to develop a new drug to treat and recover the symptoms in Satoko-chan, whose genetic profile was remarkably similar to his...``
``...So when you say Satoko is 'cooperating with the research'... you mean...?``
``I won't deny... that she is being treated as a research subject.@k@vS14/10/22100249. And I'm prepared to accept any criticism of it as inhumane.``

``But without that, there was no way to save Satoko-chan or to protect Satoshi-kun.@k@vS14/10/22100251. It's inconceivable that this disease would be covered by medical insurance, and the cost of treatment is astronomical.``
``So I... designated both of them as test subjects in order to secure government funding...!@k@vS14/10/22100253. ...I couldn't think of any other way.``
Trembling violently, Shion aimed the gun at Irie, who stood before her looking utterly defeated.
Satoshi's illness was incurable.
Unable to accept that merciless, cruel fact, tears spilled from her eyes as she sobbed. Her expression was one of despair and grief slowly being transmuted into murderous rage.
``Ah, ahahaha...!@k@vS14/06/220600125. Y-You expect me... to believe that fabricated story...?!``
``I won't be deceived... never again...!!``

``You just don't want to let go of Satoshi-kun!!@k@vS14/06/220600128. It's all about your status, your reputation!!@k@vS14/06/220600129. You want to use Satoshi-kun to satisfy your own greed!!@k@vS14/06/220600130. That's all this is, isn't iiit?!``
``Shion!!``
``Shii-chan!!``
``It's a lie... you're all liars...!!@k@vS14/06/220600132. Satoshi-kun isn't sick! He doesn't have some mysterious illness I've never heard of!!``
``When he wakes up, he'll smile again!!@k@vS14/06/220600134. He'll gently pat my head and say 'mmph' with that smile of hiiis!!``
Screaming this, Shion surged forward and jammed the gun barrel between the Coach's eyebrows. She clicked the hammer back and tightened her finger on the trigger.
``Stop it, Shion!!``
``Please stop, Shion-saaan!!``
That voice.
Footsteps, hurtling toward them from behind at tremendous speed.
We all spun around at once.
It was...
The girl who had cried out was...
``That's enough!!@k@vS14/04/220400004. That is quite enough, Shion-san!!@k@vS14/04/220400005. Please -- please, stop this!!``
Shouting this, Satoko -- her face pale and drenched in sweat, tears streaming down her cheeks -- threw herself in front of the gun barrel and spread her arms wide to shield the Coach.
Shion... no, all of us were watching the scene unfold, struggling to grasp whether what we were seeing was real.
``...S-Satoko... you're awake... how much did you...?!``
``...I heard it all...@k@vS14/04/220400007. Everything -- from the incident three years ago to my illness... all of it...!``
At those words, Rika-chan caught her breath and froze. But when Satoko turned back toward her—her eyes wide with despair—she wore a lonely smile as she spoke.
changeReason: Old uses first-person narrator ('when I looked at her') while JPN describes Satoko turning back toward Rika; the subject who acts is different.

TextENGNew: ``...Even though Rika kept it from me, I already knew.@k@vS14/04/220400009. I see it sometimes, in my dreams... memories of that day...``
``But no matter what anyone says or thinks...@k@vS14/04/220400011. ...my sin will never be washed away.``
``Because of what I did, I caused so much trouble and suffering for Nii-nii...@k@vS14/04/220400013. I truly am the worst kind of curse...!!``
``Don't say that, Satoko!@k@vS14/01/220100106. I have never, not once, thought of you that way!!@k@vS14/01/220100107. You're one of us -- our irreplaceable friend!!``
``That's right!!@k@vS14/02/220200026. Rena loves you, Satoko-chan!!@k@vS14/02/220200027. Every single day is so much more fun because you're here with us!!``
``Satoko... the villagers were so cold to you, and we couldn't do a thing about it. I'm truly sorry!@k@vS14/03/220300041. But from now on, we'll protect you with everything we have!!``
``I'll do everything in my power to help, until the day you can truly feel happy!!``

``Satoko... please don't say such sad things.@k@vS14/05/220500185. If Satoko calls herself a god of pestilence, then I refuse to believe in gods.@k@vS14/05/220500186. And besides, I'm always so happy when I'm with you, Satoko...!``
``Everyone...!``
Satoko looked genuinely, heartfully happy as she smiled, her face scrunching up as she fought back tears. Then she turned to face Shion once more.
``I truly am a hopeless person...!@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1011. But even so, I have people who encourage me with such warmth and strength...!``
``And just like all of them, the Coach has always cheered me up and worried about me...!``
``B-But...!@k@vS14/06/220600138. You're being used as a test subject...!!``
``I don't mind!@k@vS14/04/220400019. If I can be of help, that's all that matters!!``
``There are people working so hard for my happiness...!@k@vS14/04/220400021. So I want to do whatever I can for them in return!!``

``And just as everyone believes in me, I want to believe in everyone!!@k@vS14/04/220400023. It's true I was shocked to learn about Nii-nii -- but I understand there was a good reason for keeping it secret!!``
``Everyone has been looking out for me, so I won't doubt their kindness!!@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1014. My friends, the Coach...@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1015. ...and you too, Shion-san -- my Nee-nee!!!``
And then, slowly...

Satoko walked toward the frozen Shion -- who still held the gun -- and gently placed her own small hands over Shion's. Then, peering up into the dazed face looking down at her, Satoko smiled and gave a firm nod.
``Please... Nee-nee...@k@vS14/04/220400028. I get scared and anxious when I'm alone, so... will you believe together with me...?@k@vS14/04/220400029. That someday, Nii-nii will come home to us...!``
``Y-You're... okay with that...?!@k@vS14/06/220600142. Can you really bear that...?!``
No matter how long the wait.
No matter how hard she believes.
Satoshi-kun may never come home.
Even if she keeps hoping, keeps praying... those hopes may end up betrayed.
That's what Shion's eyes were asking.
But.
Satoko, with a calm and radiant smile, nodded -- firmly, decisively.
``I'm certain I can keep waiting.@k@vS14/04/220400031. ...Because Shion-san feels exactly the same way I do, and cares about me so much...@k@vS14/04/220400032. Because I have you, Nee-nee...!``
Satoko's... those words.
That face -- desperately holding back tears, fighting to keep smiling.
It slowly melted my heart, which had been on the verge of freezing over... and began to heal it.
...Even though you must want to cry too.
Even though you must want to scream, to weep even harder than me... to hurl these tangled feelings at someone.
Why do you... care so much about someone like me...?!
...Hey, Satoshi-kun.
I understand now... I finally understand.
How you were able to keep protecting this girl, no matter how much you suffered.
Satoko... our little sister... is such a brave, such a kind-hearted girl...!
``Uu... uwaaaaaahh... uwaaaaaahh...!!``

Shion's arms went limp and the gun slipped from her hands, clattering to the floor. She sank to her knees, head bowed, sobbing. When Satoko nestled close to her, Shion wrapped her arms around that small body and wept openly, loudly.
``Uwaaaaaahh... Satoko... Satokooo...!@k@vS14/06/220600145. I'm sorry... I'm so sorry! ...I... I...!!``
``...Thank you, Nee-nee...!@k@vS14/04/220400034. Thank you... for everything you've done for me...!``

``I knew... in my head, I knew...!!@k@vS14/06/220600147. But... but my heart wouldn't stop racing, and it was so painful, so sad!@k@vS14/06/220600148. I was so frustrated, so ashamed of myself... and I couldn't do anything about it...!!``
``...Yes... yes... I already knew that...!@k@vS14/04/220400036. Because my Nee-nee is a very... very kind person...!!``
``Satoko... Satokoooo...!!@k@vS14/06/220600150. Uwaaaaaahh... uwaaaaaahh!!``
Satoko buried her tear-streaked face against Shion's chest.
Before long, Shion's emotions seemed to wash over Satoko as well, and she too began to cry aloud. For a time, the two of them simply... held each other and wept.
Those were the tears... of a very, very tender pair of sisters.
